data.content:
### List Sandbox Templates (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.1/template

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with a specific team. Requires the team ID as an option.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
```

--------------------------------

### View Sandbox Logs CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.0/sandbox

Displays logs for a specific sandbox. Allows filtering by log level, following logs in real-time, and specifying output format.

```bash
e2b sandbox logs [options] <sandboxID>
```

--------------------------------

### Kill Sandbox CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.0/sandbox

Terminates a running sandbox instance. It can kill a specific sandbox using its ID or all running sandboxes with the --all option.

```bash
e2b sandbox kill [options] [sandboxID]
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.1/template

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Options allow specifying the path, config file, selecting from a list, providing a team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Spawn Sandbox CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.0/sandbox

Creates a new sandbox instance and connects a terminal to it. Supports specifying a template and changing the execution path.

```bash
e2b sandbox spawn [options] [template]
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.1/template

Creates a basic E2B Dockerfile (./e2b.Dockerfile) in the root directory, which can then be used to build a sandbox template. An option to specify the path is available.

```bash
e2b template init [options]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to Sandbox CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.0/sandbox

Connects a terminal to an already running sandbox instance. Requires the sandbox ID as an argument.

```bash
e2b sandbox connect [options] <sandboxID>
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI using Homebrew or NPM

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sandbox-template

Installs the E2B Command Line Interface (CLI) tool. This is the first step to manage and build sandbox templates. It can be installed via Homebrew on macOS or NPM globally.

```bash
brew install e2b
```

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### E2B Sandbox CLI Usage

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.0/sandbox

The base command for interacting with E2B sandboxes. This command serves as an entry point for various sandbox management operations.

```bash
e2b sandbox [options] [command]
```

--------------------------------

### Unpublish Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.1/template

Unpublishes a sandbox template, making it unavailable. Options include specifying the path, config file, selecting from a list, providing a team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template unpublish [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual unpublish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI with NPM

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/cli

Installs the E2B CLI globally using NPM. This command ensures the CLI is accessible from any directory in your terminal.

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI with Homebrew (macOS)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/cli

Installs the E2B CLI using the Homebrew package manager on macOS. This is a straightforward command to add the CLI to your system's PATH.

```bash
brew install e2b
```

--------------------------------

### CLI Browser Authentication

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/cli/auth

Initiates browser-based authentication for the E2B CLI. This method requires an interactive browser environment and opens the default browser for user login.

```bash
e2b auth login
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template with Ready Command (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sandbox-template/ready-cmd

This command builds a sandbox template and specifies a custom ready command using the `--ready-cmd` option. The command provided will be executed to determine when the sandbox is ready for a snapshot.

```bash
e2b template build --ready-cmd "<your-ready-command>"
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.1/template

Builds a sandbox template from a Dockerfile (e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile) in the root directory. It also generates an e2b.toml configuration file. Options allow specifying the path, Dockerfile, template name, start command, team ID, config file, CPU count, memory, and build arguments.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory.
  -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores.
  -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started.
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2.
  --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512.
  --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>.
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI: Manage Sandbox Templates

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.3.0/template

The `e2b template` command is the primary interface for managing sandbox templates. It supports various subcommands for template lifecycle operations.

```bash
e2b template [options] [command]
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.2.1/template

Deletes a specified sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Options include specifying the path, config file, selecting from a list, providing a team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Dockerfile with E2B CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.3.0/template

Creates a basic `e2b.Dockerfile` in the root directory, which can then be used to build a sandbox template. An optional path can be specified to change the execution directory.

```bash
e2b template init [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Get Sandbox Metrics using CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sandbox/metrics

Retrieve sandbox resource utilization metrics directly from the command line. This command requires the sandbox ID as an argument and outputs formatted metrics including CPU, memory, and disk usage. The output is human-readable and updated periodically.

```bash
e2b sandbox metrics <sandbox_id> 

# Metrics for sandbox <sandbox_id>
# 
# [2025-07-25 14:05:55Z]  CPU:  8.27% /  2 Cores | Memory:    31 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
# [2025-07-25 14:06:00Z]  CPU:   0.5% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
# [2025-07-25 14:06:05Z]  CPU:   0.1% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
# [2025-07-25 14:06:10Z]  CPU:   0.3% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI Authentication Usage

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.10/auth

Displays the general usage instructions for the e2b auth command, including available subcommands and options.

```bash
e2b auth [options] [command]
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI: Initialize Sandbox Template

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.0.2/template

Creates a basic E2B Dockerfile (e2b.Dockerfile) in the root directory. This serves as a starting point for building a new sandbox template.

```bash
e2b template init [options]

```

--------------------------------

### Shutdown All Sandboxes using E2B CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/cli/shutdown-sandboxes

This command terminates all currently running sandboxes managed by the E2B CLI. Use this command with caution as it will stop all active sandboxes. It's a quick way to clear all running environments.

```bash
e2b sandbox kill --all
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.0/template

Lists available sandbox templates. Options allow specifying the team ID and the output format (e.g., json, pretty).

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -f, --format <format>: output format, eg. json, pretty
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI Authentication Commands Usage

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.2/auth

Provides the general usage syntax for the e2b auth command and its subcommands. This is the entry point for all authentication-related operations.

```bash
e2b auth [options] [command]

```

--------------------------------

### CLI Environment Variable Authentication

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/cli/auth

Sets environment variables for E2B CLI authentication. This method uses API keys and access tokens, which can be managed via the E2B API Key page.

```bash
export E2B_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
export E2B_ACCESS_TOKEN=your_acces_token_here
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.2/template

Builds a sandbox template using either './e2b.Dockerfile' or './Dockerfile' in the root directory. This command also generates an 'e2b.toml' configuration file. Options allow specifying the root path, Dockerfile, template name, start command, ready command, team ID, config file, CPU count, memory, build arguments, and cache behavior.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory.
  -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores.
  -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started.
  --ready-cmd <ready-command>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready.
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with.
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2.
  --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512.
  --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>.
  --no-cache: skip cache when building the template.
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI Logout Command Usage

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.2/auth

Explains the usage of the 'e2b auth logout' command, used to log out of the CLI. This command typically does not require arguments but may support options.

```bash
e2b auth logout [options]

```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI: Delete Sandbox Template

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.0.2/template

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Options allow specifying the path, selecting from a list, or skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox with Start Command (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sandbox-template/start-cmd

This command builds a sandbox template and specifies a start command to be executed automatically when the sandbox is spawned. Replace '<your-start-command>' with the actual command you want to run.

```bash
e2b template build -c "<your-start-command>"
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate E2B Template using CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/template/migration-v2

This command initiates the migration process for an existing E2B template. It renames the original e2b.toml and e2b.Dockerfile to .old versions and generates new files for the template definition and build scripts based on the selected language.

```bash
e2b template migrate
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template with E2B CLI (v2)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.10/template

Builds a Dockerfile as a Sandbox template directly. This command reads a Dockerfile and builds it. Options allow specifying the path, Dockerfile, start command, ready command, CPU count, memory, and cache behavior.

```bash
e2b template build-v2 [options] <template-name>

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory.
  -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started.
  --ready-cmd <ready-cmd>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready.
  --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2.
  --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512.
  --no-cache: skip cache when building the template.
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI Login Command

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.10/auth

Command to log in to the E2B CLI. It typically accepts options to facilitate the authentication process.

```bash
e2b auth login [options]
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI: List Sandbox Templates

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.0.2/template

Lists available sandbox templates. This command can be filtered by a team ID to show templates associated with a specific team.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

```

--------------------------------

### View Sandbox Metrics

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.9.2/sandbox

Fetches and displays performance metrics for a sandbox. Supports following the metrics stream and custom output formats.

```bash
e2b sandbox metrics [options] <sandboxID>
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI Logout Command

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.10/auth

Command to log out of the E2B CLI. It may accept options to clear session tokens or other authentication-related data.

```bash
e2b auth logout [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.3.0/template

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated `e2b.toml` configuration file. Options include specifying the path, config file, selecting from an interactive list, providing a team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI: Build Sandbox Template

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.8.0/template

Builds a sandbox template using a Dockerfile. It can be configured with various options like specifying the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, and resource allocation. It also generates an e2b.toml config file.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory.
  -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores.
  -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started.
  --ready-cmd <ready-command>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready.
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with.
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2.
  --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512.
  --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>.
  --no-cache: skip cache when building the template.
```

--------------------------------

### CLI: e2b sandbox metrics <sandbox_id>

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sandbox/metrics

Retrieve sandbox metrics directly from the command line interface. This command provides a human-readable output of the sandbox's resource utilization.

```APIDOC
## CLI: e2b sandbox metrics <sandbox_id>

### Description
Retrieve sandbox metrics directly from the command line interface. This command provides a human-readable output of the sandbox's resource utilization.

### Method
CLI Command

### Endpoint
e2b sandbox metrics <sandbox_id>

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandbox_id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```bash
e2b sandbox metrics <sandbox_id>
```

### Response
#### Success Response
- **Output** (string) - Human-readable metrics including CPU, Memory, and Disk usage.

#### Response Example
```
Metrics for sandbox <sandbox_id>

[2025-07-25 14:05:55Z]  CPU:  8.27% /  2 Cores | Memory:    31 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
[2025-07-25 14:06:00Z]  CPU:   0.5% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
[2025-07-25 14:06:05Z]  CPU:   0.1% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
[2025-07-25 14:06:10Z]  CPU:   0.3% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
```

### Notes
It may take a second or more to get the first metrics after the sandbox is created. Until the first metrics are collected from the sandbox, an empty array may be returned.
```

--------------------------------

### Create E2B Sandbox

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.2/sandbox

Creates a new sandbox and connects a terminal to it. Allows specifying a template and a custom path or configuration file.

```bash
e2b sandbox create [options] [template]
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Sandbox Template with E2B CLI (v2)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.10/template

Initializes a new sandbox template using the SDK. Options allow specifying the path, template name (alias), and target language (typescript, python-sync, python-async).

```bash
e2b template init-v2 [options]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -n, --name <name>: template name (alias)
  -l, --language <language>: target language: typescript, python-sync, python-async
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI Login Command Usage

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.2/auth

Details the usage of the 'e2b auth login' command, which is used to log in to the CLI. It may accept various options to facilitate the login process.

```bash
e2b auth login [options]

```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Sandbox Start Command Logs (CLI)

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/sandbox-template/start-cmd

This command retrieves the logs generated by the start command during the build phase of a specific sandbox. Replace '<sandbox-id>' with the actual ID of your sandbox.

```bash
e2b sandbox logs <sandbox-id>
```

--------------------------------

### Shutdown Specific Sandboxes using E2B CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/cli/shutdown-sandboxes

This command allows you to terminate one or more specific sandboxes by providing their unique IDs. Ensure you have the correct sandbox IDs before execution. This is useful for managing individual sandbox lifecycles.

```bash
e2b sandbox kill <sandbox-id1> <sandbox-id2> <sandbox-id3>
```

--------------------------------

### Shutdown Sandboxes by State and Metadata using E2B CLI

Source: https://e2b.dev/docs/cli/shutdown-sandboxes

This command allows for selective shutdown of sandboxes based on their state (e.g., running, paused) and metadata. You can combine state and metadata filters for precise control. This is useful for automated cleanup or targeted management.

```bash
e2b sandbox kill --all --state=running,paused
```

```bash
e2b sandbox kill --all --metadata=key=value
```

```bash
e2b sandbox kill --all --state=running,paused --metadata=key=value
```
